text:
BOTTOM LINE — Semis lead, energy fades.  
SOXX’s +5.45% surge flipped a risk-on tape despite VIX at 17.1; small caps lagged large by 0.40pp as positioning rotated into tech. The day’s thesis: a two-week selloff in semiconductors triggered oversold buying, crushing energy’s 20d uptrend.

INDICES — VIX 17.1, -2.68% vs 20d. SOXX +5.45%, QQQ +1.85%, IWM +1.45%, SPY +0.83%, DIA +0.69%. Small > large by 0.40pp. Silver jumped +4.12%, TLT fell -0.27%. Neither SPY nor QQQ is below 200-day MA.

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio 0.8x on +0.83% = low-conviction rally; QQQ vol_ratio 0.9x on +1.85% = light participation. GXC exploded at 16.4x volume (+0.07%), showing interest but no price follow-through. SOXX elevated at 1.1x with price surge = accumulation in semis.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLK Technology +2.89%, XLE Energy +0.97%, XLV Healthcare +0.63%, XLI Industrials +0.30%, XLY Discretionary +0.23%, XLB Materials +0.14%, XLF Financials +0.12%, XLU Utilities -0.04%, XLRE Real Estate -0.07%, XLC Comms -0.69%, XLP Staples -0.94%. Top 2 Industries: DRAM +10.91%, WGMI +6.47%; bottom 2: CIBR -1.32%, KWEB -1.53%. Money moved into semiconductor hardware (DRAM, SOXX) and out of defensive staples and Chinese tech; rotation state: AI hardware leading.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR_vol 1.23 elevated = protection demand; QQQ PCR_vol 2.16 extreme = heavy put buying. SPY GEX flip at 758.0 vs spot ~745.1 — price below gamma flip, amplifying downside if selling resumes. VIX at 17.1 above its 20-day MA of 16.9 = elevated but not extreme fear.

CROSS-ASSET — Silver +4.12% and gold +1.96% rallied alongside semis — unusual risk-on precious metals divergence from energy. Oil +2.66% but XLE only +0.97%, hinting at profit-taking in energy names.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed 65.4 Greed. Top movers: DHR -10.99% (Medtech, vol 7.0x distribution), MSCI -10.14% (Toll Road, vol 2.9x distribution), SOXX +5.45% (semis, accumulation). Watch: DHR (7.0x vol spike + -10.99% = heavy distribution, trend break), GXC (16.4x vol near flat price = abnormal interest, potential breakout), MSCI (2.9x vol on -10.14% = capitulation, test of 20d support).
